name: Coda API
what_it_does: Coda's REST API for reading and writing to Coda documents, tables, rows, columns, and controls — combining document and database capabilities in one platform.
use_cases: Reading and updating Coda table rows from agent workflows
Automating data entry into Coda databases
Generating Coda pages with agent-produced content
Syncing external data sources into Coda tables
Building approval workflows that interact with Coda forms and tables
not_for: High-frequency real-time data updates (better suited to dedicated databases)
Complex relational data requiring SQL-style joins
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Coda as a collaborative workspace, particularly reading from or writing to structured Coda tables.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't use Coda, or you need sub-second latency data operations.

auth.notes: Simple API token authentication. Tokens are workspace-scoped. No OAuth flow required — very easy for agents to use. Tokens are created in Coda account settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan with limited rows and features
pricing.notes: API access on all plans. Free plan limits rows and automation runs. Pro plan needed for most automation use cases.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include status code and message. Less detailed than HubSpot or Stripe. Missing error on non-existent row requires checking status 404.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Row creation will create duplicates if retried. Agents should upsert by querying before inserting.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 100 req/min is tight for agents processing many rows
Row IDs are not stable — rows can be reordered and IDs may change
No native upsert — agents must query then create or update
OpenAPI spec exists but generated SDK is not officially maintained
Webhook setup requires a publicly accessible URL
Formula columns are read-only via API
